title: Quantum integrable matrix models of spinor Bose gases in one spatial dimension
abstract: Degenerate spinor Bose gases with repulsive density-density interaction and anti-ferromagnetic spin-spin coupling in one spatial dimension are shown to be described by a quantum integrable matrix extension of the nonlinear Schrödinger model, whose fundamental fields are described by an $m\,\times\,n$ matrix of bosonic field operators. The eigenstates of this model are constructed for arbitrarily sized matrix field operators by means of algebraic Bethe-ansatz techniques, and the corresponding Bethe equations governing the spectra of conserved quantities are derived. The approach thus generalizes previously chosen techniques to account for arbitrary spin multiplets and their spin-spin interaction. Focusing on the specific case of the $2\times2$ model, which is shown to correspond to a spin-$1$ Bose gas, a set of integral equations is derived, which describe its equilibrium thermodynamic properties. From these, the ground state phase diagram is computed both, numerically and analytically in the parameter plane spanned by the chemical potential and an external magnetic field. Furthermore, the existence of paired bound states is shown to modify the Pauli exclusion principle for interacting bosons in one dimension. In particular, it is found that no two quasiparticle rapidities can coincide, provided that the Lieb parameter satisfies $γ>4/3$.
summary: This paper develops a mathematical framework using matrix-valued bosonic fields to describe 1D spinor Bose gases with arbitrary spin. It successfully maps the spin-1 case to a set of solvable integral equations, allowing for the precise calculation of the ground state phase diagram. The work highlights how interactions can lead to bound states that change the effective exclusion principle for the particles.
main_problem: The paper aims to generalize the description of one-dimensional spinor Bose gases to arbitrary spin multiplets using a matrix-based integrable model.
main_result: The authors derive a quantum integrable matrix extension of the nonlinear Schrödinger model and compute the ground state phase diagram for a spin-1 Bose gas, revealing that paired bound states modify the effective Pauli exclusion principle for bosons.
method: The study employs algebraic Bethe-ansatz techniques to construct eigenstates and derive Bethe equations, alongside the derivation of integral equations for thermodynamic properties.
model_or_system: A quantum integrable matrix extension of the nonlinear Schrödinger model where fundamental fields are represented by an m x n matrix of bosonic field operators, specifically applied to spin-1 Bose gases with repulsive density-density and anti-ferromagnetic spin-spin interactions.
key_observables: Ground state phase diagram, spectra of conserved quantities, and equilibrium thermodynamic properties.
important_parameters: Chemical potential, external magnetic field, and the Lieb parameter (gamma).
main_assumptions: The system is restricted to one spatial dimension with repulsive density-density interaction and anti-ferromagnetic spin-spin coupling.
paper_structure: The paper introduces a matrix extension of the nonlinear Schrödinger model, applies the algebraic Bethe-ansatz to find eigenstates and Bethe equations, focuses on the spin-1 case to derive thermodynamic integral equations, and concludes with a phase diagram analysis and discussion of bound state effects.
why_interesting: It provides a powerful integrable framework for studying complex spin multiplets in 1D, offering new insights into how bound states can alter the fundamental statistics (Pauli exclusion principle) of interacting bosons in cold atom systems.
